faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Check here first — most issues resolve quickly
faq.entryHint: Check the FAQ before submitting — it may solve your issue faster

connection.question: What should I do about unstable connection or slow speed?
connection.answer:
Speed issues are usually related to your local network. Please troubleshoot in this order:

1. Try different tunnel lines first. If all lines are slow, it's almost certainly a local network issue.
2. Restart your router. This resolves most temporary routing cache and link issues.
3. Call your ISP and ask whether they've applied targeted throttling for overseas traffic.
4. If the ISP offers to send a technician on-site, decline — ISPs often have "broadband processing" KPIs and will push you to downgrade your plan by citing fake "line faults".
5. Only after ruling out the above is it possibly a node issue. Submit feedback with logs and we'll investigate.

appNotWorking.question: A specific app or game stopped working after I installed Kaitu — what now?
appNotWorking.answer:
Some apps (notably Adobe Creative Cloud, certain games, and third-party IM tools) cache their own server addresses. When Kaitu is on, those cached addresses get routed through the proxy and may be rejected or time out. Try in this order:

1. Restart the app — most of the time, quitting and reopening lets it refresh its server cache.
2. Switch routing mode — toggle between Smart and Global in Kaitu, then try the app again.
3. Temporarily disconnect Kaitu — in rare cases (e.g. Adobe Creative Cloud caching a now-defunct legacy IP), disconnect Kaitu while using that app and reconnect afterwards.
4. If none of the above helps, submit a ticket with logs and our engineers will investigate.

nodeChoice.question: There are so many nodes — which one should I pick?
nodeChoice.answer:
Easiest option: use Auto Select. Tap "Auto Select" at the top of the node list and Kaitu will pick the best node based on real-time latency and load. Turn on "Auto Switch" and it'll move you to a better node when conditions change. This is the best choice for most users.

If you really want to pick manually:

• Don't always tap the first node — the first few (especially AU / Australia) get hammered because most users tap the first one by reflex.
• Try JP / SG / KR / US first — usually more stable and faster.
• If it won't connect or feels slow, switch to a different one — the best node varies by region and time of day.
• Streaming and browsing aren't sensitive to node choice; gaming and video calls are latency-sensitive, so try a few and stick with the most stable.

wifiSwitch.question: VPN disconnects after switching WiFi or network?
wifiSwitch.answer: Kaitu supports automatic reconnection after network changes, but the trigger has a delay of several seconds to a dozen seconds. If you don't want to wait, manually tap Disconnect then Connect to recover immediately.

deviceRemoved.question: Why is my device being removed / kicked out?
deviceRemoved.answer:
Each account supports up to 5 devices logged in simultaneously. When you log in on a 6th device, the system automatically removes the earliest logged-in device.

If you're certain you haven't exceeded 5 devices but are still being kicked, please submit feedback and we'll help check your account's device bindings.

updateIssue.question: App doesn't work after update?
updateIssue.answer:
Update mechanisms differ across platforms, and plugins sometimes don't reload immediately:

• Windows / macOS: Restart your computer so the new version takes full effect.
• iOS: If you see issues after update, delete the app and reinstall from the App Store — this resolves Network Extension plugin reload issues.
• Android: Restart your phone, or force-stop the app in Settings and reopen it.

loginFailed.question: Login failed / verification code not received?
loginFailed.answer:
The most common cause is a typo in the email — about 30% of users in our database mistype their email on first attempt. Please check letter by letter:

• Spaces or extra characters around the @ sign
• Domain spelling (e.g. gmial → gmail, hotmial → hotmail)
• Confusion between 0 (zero) and O (letter)

After confirming, check your spam folder. If nothing arrives, try a different email address.

allNationConnect.question: I used to use AllNationConnect — can I log in with Kaitu?
allNationConnect.answer:
Yes. AllNationConnect is the earlier name of our Kaitu client and is no longer maintained (no protocol updates, no new nodes). Please:

1. Search "Kaitu" on the App Store / Google Play, or visit kaitu.io to download the new client.
2. Log in with your original email — your subscription syncs automatically, no need to repurchase.
3. If you hit stability issues on the old client, upgrade to the new Kaitu — the old version no longer receives fixes.

chinaAppStore.question: How is Kaitu listed on the App Store?
chinaAppStore.answer:
Our iOS client is designed within a compliant framework and passed Apple's standard review process for listing.

If other developers would like to discuss iOS listing experiences, feel free to contact us by email — we're happy to share.

ticket.title: Submit Ticket
ticket.description: Describe your issue and we'll reply via email as soon as possible.
ticket.contentPlaceholder: Please describe the issue in detail, including operation steps, error messages, etc., which will help us resolve the issue faster.
ticket.submitFailed: Failed to submit ticket, please try again later
ticket.emailReplyHint: Our support team will communicate with you via email, please watch for replies in your inbox.
ticket.dataCollectionHint: App version, system info, and logs are automatically collected on submission to help us diagnose issues faster.
ticket.entryDescription: Encountered a problem? Submit a ticket, we'll reply soon
ticket.betaWarning: You are using a Beta version. If you experience issues, you can switch back to stable in Account settings.

ticket.anonymousHint: You are not logged in. We will reply to you via email after submission.
